description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with a single build stage that runs on Docker runners (tagged "docker") and uses the shared artifact definition (logs, expire in 3 weeks, always). Define hidden jobs named .gcc-10_SDL_SDL-1.2_old_sanitize, .gcc-10_SDL_SDL-1.2_old_sanitize_test, .gcc-10_SDL_SDL-1.2_sanitize, .gcc-10_SDL_SDL-1.2_sanitize2_test, .gcc-10_SDL_SDL-1.2_sanitize_test, .gcc-10_SDL_default_old_sanitize, .gcc-10_SDL_default_old_sanitize_test, .gcc-10_SDL_default_sanitize, .gcc-10_SDL_default_sanitize2_test, .gcc-10_SDL_default_sanitize_test, and .gcc-10_zlib_ng_develop; each job runs the script sequence: download the library with ./tools/ci/scripts/downloadlib.sh ${LIBNAME} ${LIBVERSION}, optionally echo the runenv script, invoke /usr/local/spm/bin/${LIBNAME}_${LIBVERSION}/runenv.sh ${runenvx} ${runenvz} env, then execute the appropriate build or test script (e.g., ./tools/ci/jobs/gcc10_sanitize.sh, ./tools/ci/jobs/gcc10_sanitize_tests.sh with flags like --enable-unittests=yes, --without-manaplusgame, --with-sdl2, etc.), run ./tools/ci/scripts/runtests.sh or ./tools/ci/scripts/checkldd.sh on the built binaries, and finally verify ASan linkage with ldd ... | grep "libasan"; test jobs also set LD_PRELOAD and may export ASAN_OPTIONS and JOBS. All jobs inherit the same artifact settings, have empty dependencies, and are excluded from trigger pipelines via an "except: - triggers" rule. Each job defines its own variables such as LIBNAME, LIBVERSION, PACKAGES, runenvx, runenvz, and optionally POST_CXXFLAGS, ASAN_OPTIONS, or JOBS, but no deployment or publishing steps are configured.
